title: Plain-language public explainer (education-only; "not medical advice")
context: GATED: This milestone is attempted ONLY if an oncologist + patient-advocate reviewer pair is secured. If that pair is not secured, this task is formally skipped and the project ships M2-M3 outputs only. When attempted: this is HIGH risk (patient-/public-facing health content). All content is education-only, carries a persistent 'not medical advice' label, and requires blocking oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off before merge. No screening, prevention, or treatment recommendations to individuals. Depends on: disparities-analysis-report-012.
objective: Write a plain-language, education-only public explainer on the exemplar site's cancer disparities for a community/public audience. The explainer: uses plain language (accessible reading level); is framed entirely as general education; carries a persistent 'This is general education, not medical advice — consult your care team' label on every page/section; gives no screening, prevention, or treatment recommendation to any individual; uses non-stigmatizing, asset-based, community-centered language per the editorial policy; states findings with evidence-tiered structural framing; includes the standard caveats; sources all facts; and has no ranking/scorecard content. Blocking oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off required before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Written at an accessible reading level appropriate for a general community audience; technical terms are explained or avoided
Carries a persistent 'This is general education, not medical advice — consult your care team' label visible on every page or section — not only in a footnote
Gives no screening, prevention, or treatment recommendation to any individual; does not advise any person about their own care
Uses non-stigmatizing, asset-based, person-first, community-centered language throughout per disparities-analysis-framing-001; no deficit framing
States findings with evidence-tiered structural framing: 'this analysis measures the gap' is distinguished from 'the established literature attributes such gaps to structural factors X/Y (cite)'
Includes standard caveats (data vintage, suppression, 'association is not causation', 'adjusted-for / not-adjusted-for') in accessible language
All facts are sourced; citation-coverage CI check passes
No ranking/scorecard content; no 'worst places' or naming-and-shaming language
Oncologist sign-off recorded before merge (blocking): clinical accuracy, 'not medical advice' framing, and safety of any health-related content verified
Patient-advocate sign-off recorded before merge (blocking): community framing, language, and appropriateness verified
output: A reviewed plain-language public explainer document (education-only, 'not medical advice') for the exemplar cancer site, with blocking oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off recorded before merge.
